pattern: A sliding window with a last-seen map lets the left edge jump past any repeat in a single linear pass.
hints: Think of a window that always holds distinct characters; what must happen to it when a repeat arrives?
Remember the most recent index of every character so you can move the window's left edge in one step instead of crawling it forward.

Walk a right pointer over the string keeping a dict of char -> last index. When the current char was seen at or after the window's start, snap the start to just past that index, then track the largest window width.

edge_inputs.description: all identical characters, answer is one not the length
repeat sits before the window start so it must not shrink
digits, symbols and spaces all count as characters
best window is in the middle, not at either end

stress.description: long random string over a small alphabet so repeats are frequent
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    alphabet = 'abcdefgh'
    return [''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))]
stress.note: small alphabet forces many window resets; reference must stay linear
